title: 9 Returning Cheques on ORS
body_text:
9	Returning Cheques on ORS
9.1	Overview of Returning Cheques on ORS
During enrollment to ORS, your financial institution determines if it will enter cheque returns directly on ORS or upload files to ORS. This Chapter describes how to dishonour and return Canadian and US dollar Incoming Clearing Items directly on ORS. For procedures on uploading cheque return files to Central 1, see Chapter 14, Uploading Files to ORS. The Dishonour and Return of Cheques Part provides guidance on the business rules surrounding cheque returns and describes how to return cheques manually. Manual returns are only permitted if
the cheque is an Unqualified Item (use ORS to return Qualified Incoming Clearing Items),
the cheque cleared before images were available on ORS,
the cheque cannot be returned using ORS (such as a misdirected Chargeback), or
ORS is unavailable.
Financial institutions use ORS to validate Incoming Clearing Items (e.g., verify the Drawerâs signature, check for post-dated or stale-dated items, etc.). Physical cheques arenât returned to your financial institution. Therefore, financial institution staff can use ORS to make pay or no-pay decisions and enter their cheque returns. See Section 1.2, ORS Service Windows, Image Availability, and Archive, for information on the availability of cheque images in ORS.
Once returned cheques are entered on ORS or a file of returned cheques is uploaded to ORS, Central 1 forwards the dishonoured cheques to the negotiating financial institutions using Return Replacement Documents (RRD) or Image Printouts.
BC financial institutions use ORS to return cheques cleared on or after October 1, 2010. Although cheque images are available for items cleared prior to October 1, 2010, this was the date that Central 1 implemented Payments Canada Standard 013, Return Replacement Document Design Standard. Return cheques manually if they cleared prior to October 1, 2010.
Ontario financial institutions use ORS to return US dollar cheques cleared April 5, 2011, or later and Canadian dollar cheques cleared April 2, 2012, or later. Return cheques manually if they cleared prior to the previously noted dates.
Your financial institution is responsible for complying with all Payments Canada rules and time frames. Central 1 doesnât process returned item requests that donât comply with Payments Canada rules. Non-compliant returns are charged back to your financial institution.
Related Information
Section 1.2
, ORS Service Windows, Image Availability, and Archive
Chapter 14
, Uploading Files to ORS
9.2	Identifying Cheques Eligible for Return on ORS
The ORS search results are colour-coded to help users identify cheques that are eligible for return. The eligibility colour code is different than the service window colour code. The eligibility colour code describes whether the item selected for return will be processed during a regular or Payments Canada late return cycle. The service window colour code identifies whether the user is working in a regular or research service window on ORS.
The colour codes in the following table describe whether the item is processed during a regular or Payments Canada late return cycle (and therefore whether the item is eligible for return). See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Eligibility Colour Codes (Return Cycles)
Green (on time)
â
Cheques are in the normal return cycle and can be returned.
Red (Payments Canada late)
â Cheques arenât in the normal return cycle and can only be returned following Payments Canada rules for extended time frames (i.e., only specific return reason codes are allowed). ORS enforces compliance with these rules.
The eligibility colour code is displayed under the A column. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Returned Item Colour Codes
Related Information
Section 1.2
, ORS Service Windows, Image Availability, and Archive
Appendix #A-5
, Cheque Return Reason Codes
9.3	Returning Cheques on ORS
Proceed as follows to return a cheque on ORS:
Log in to ORS and locate the cheque that you want to return. See Section 7.2, Searching for Cheques on ORS, for procedures.
Select the
E
link in the A column beside the cheque that you want to return. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Search Results
Note:	The E link is also available on the Search View page.
Complete the Edit Cheque Return page as follows. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Edit Cheque Return Page
â
Posting Transit
â To post the credit for the return (displayed as XR) to a branch other than the one from which the cheque is drawn, select a Transit Number from the drop-down list. If you donât select a posting transit, the returned item will be credited to the branch on which the cheque is drawn.
â
Reason Code 1
â Enter a 3-digit return reason code or select a return reason from the drop-down list.
Important
:	See Appendix #A-5, Cheque Return Reason Codes, for a summary of the Payments Canada return time frames and business rules that apply to each return reason code. Some return reasons require a statutory declaration or a reimbursement claim. See the Dishonour and Return of Cheques Part for additional information.
Select
Submit
. The return status appears in the RI column. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Search Results with Return Information
Note: 	The status of the cheque will be displayed on the Search View page if this is the page from which the E link was selected.
P
= Pending
C
= Complete
F
= Forward
X
= Cancelled
The service window during which the cheque was returned appears in the TF column.
XR
= Regular Service window (before 6:00 pm PT/9:00 pm ET)
XX
= Emergency Service window (must be activated by Central 1)
Related Information
Appendix #A-5,
Cheque Return Reason Codes
9.4	Editing Cheque Returns on ORS
Cheque return information (e.g., the return reason code) can be edited on ORS during regular service windows.
Only cheques with an RI column status of Pending (P) can be edited. If the RI column has a status of Complete (C), the item has been returned to the Drawee financial institution and the E link isnât available.
Proceed as follows to edit a cheque return on ORS:
Log in to ORS and select the
Summary
link on the ORS menu bar. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Summary Option
Select the
E
link beside the returned cheque that you want to edit. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Return Summary â Current
Note:	The E link is also available on the Search Results and Search View pages.
Make the necessary changes (e.g., select a different return reason code). See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Edit Cheque Return
Select
Submit
to save the change.
Related Information
Section 1.2
, ORS Service Windows, Image Availability, and Archive
9.5	Viewing a Summary of Cheque Returns on ORS
Proceed as follows to view a summary of a cheque returned on ORS:
Log in to ORS and select the
Summary
link on the ORS menu bar. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Summary Option
View the cheques selected for return on the current date. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Return Summary â Current
Note:	Items can be edited directly from the Return Summary page by selecting the
E
link in the A column.
9.6	Cancelling Cheque Returns on ORS
Cheque returns can be cancelled on ORS during the regular service windows. See Section 1.2, ORS Service Windows, Image Availability, and Archive.
Only cheques with an RI column status of Pending (P) can be cancelled. If the RI column has a status of Complete (C), the item has been returned to the Drawee financial institution and the E link isnât available.
Proceed as follows to cancel a cheque return on ORS:
Log in to ORS and select the
Summary
link on the ORS menu bar. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Summary Option
Select the
E
link beside the returned cheque that you want to cancel. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Return Summary â Current
Note:	The E link is also accessible from the Search Results or Search View pages.
Select
delete
. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: Delete Cheque
Related Information
Section 1.2
, ORS Service Windows, Image Availability, and Archive
9.7	Settlement of Returned Cheques
Canadian dollar cheque returns are credited to your financial institutionâs Canadian dollar Central 1 account. US dollar returns are credited to your financial institutionâs US dollar Central 1 account.
Review the ORPI â Online Returns Processed Items Report (for Canadian Dollar items) and the ORUI â USD Online Returns Processed Items Report (for US Dollar items) daily to verify that all requested returns were processed. Notify Central 1 immediately if a discrepancy is identified. See the Dishonour and Return of Cheques Part for more information on the ORPI and ORUI reports.
Related Information
Section 1.3
, Contacts and Websites
END
